BOK Gov: At Least Two More Rate Cuts Could Be Possible This Year
China Refuses to Play U.S. Tariff Game
data.description:
Beijing has dismissed the U.S.'s 'tariff numbers game,' China's foreign ministry said on Thursday, following the White House's outline of how China's retaliatory actions result in tariffs of up to 245%.

The U.S. breakdown includes a 125% reciprocal tariff, 20% fentanyl-related duty, and 7.5%–100% tariffs targeting unfair trade practices.

President Trump had recently imposed new tariffs globally, but later eased them for many countries, excluding China.

In response, Beijing raised its own tariffs, insisting negotiations must be based on mutual respect.

China also filed a WTO complaint last week, accusing the U.S. of rule violations.

Amid rising tensions, Beijing unexpectedly replaced its top trade negotiator, appointing Li Chenggang, its WTO envoy, to succeed Wang Shouwen.

Meanwhile, Washington stated Trump is open to a deal, but that Beijing must take the first step, adding China “needs our money.” Other countries are now exploring bilateral trade pacts with the U.S.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Asian Stocks Rise as U.S.-Japan Trade Talks Kick Off

The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.6%, with electronics and heavy industry stocks leading gains.

The Post-Covid Era of Ultra-Calm Markets Is Over

Investors are abandoning go-to strategies like "buy the dip" and snapping up bearish bets, bracing for more volatility.

ECB Is Poised for Seventh Rate Cut. Why It's Easing Faster than the Fed.

The European Central Bank is expected to reduce its benchmark lending rate for the seventh time since it started lowering borrowing costs in June.

Delisting Chinese Stocks Is a Real Possibility for Trump. There's a Lot at Stake.

Alibaba and Baidu, which both trade on the Nasdaq, have tumbled on worries that they could be banned from the U.S.

Foreign Central Banks Are Loading Up on T-Bills, Selling U.S. Bonds

Every month, the Treasury Department publishes delayed data on U.S. assets sold and bought by foreign central banks and investors.

Powell Warns of 'Challenging Scenario' for Fed as Trade War Rages

The central bank is wary of a situation where prices rise at the same time that growth slumps.

U.S. Tries to Crush China's AI Ambitions With Chips Crackdown

New chip restrictions for Nvidia and AMD show the Trump administration's determination to battle China on tech advances as well as trade.

Japan's Exports Rose in March, But Pace Slowed Sharply

Japan's exports rose for a sixth straight month in March but at a much slower pace as concerns over U.S. President Trump's tariffs start to weigh on global trade.

Bank of Korea Keeps Rates on Hold as Tariff Uncertainty Rises

South Korea's central bank held interest rates steady in April, taking a widely anticipated breather as it gauges how best to support an economy facing a severe hit from U.S. tariffs.

New Zealand's Annual Inflation Rises More Than Expected

New Zealand consumer prices rose more than expected in the first quarter, pushing annual inflation higher for the first time in almost three years just as the central bank faces tariff-driven economic uncertainty.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Taiwan Semi Earnings Are Coming. Guidance Is Key.

TSMC is a crucial player in the artificial-intelligence chip market, which has been upended by tariff turmoil.

Alcoa to Face Steep Costs From Tariffs Despite Strong Results

The aluminum producer said it incurred about $20 million in first-quarter costs stemming from tariffs on imports. Alcoa's CEO said it would be impractical in the short term to increase U.S. aluminum production.

BHP's Third-Quarter Copper Production Rises, Iron Ore Output Flat

The world's No. 1 miner by market value reported a 10% rise in third-quarter copper output due to higher volumes from the giant Escondida mine, and flat iron-ore output.

Brunello Cucinelli Reports Revenue Growth, Confirms Guidance

The Italian luxury-fashion firm said its results were in line with expectations, and that revenue continued on a solid upward trend in the first half of April.

Moncler Beats Views but Signals Unstable Macroeconomic Environment

Moncler's update comes days after luxury bellwether LVMH reported lackluster results for the start of the year, setting a negative tone for the reporting season.

Nvidia Is Now the Biggest U.S.-China Bargaining Chip

The ban on sales of the H20 chips calls into question the company's ability to continually beat Wall Street's lofty expectations.

Hertz Shares Jump 56% as Billionaire Investor Bill Ackman Discloses Stake

Pershing Square Capital Management says it now owns about 4.1% of the company.

Heineken Tops Revenue View, Reaffirms Outlook Amid Tariff Concerns

The Dutch brewer backed its full-year guidance after quarterly performance beat market expectations, but warned that the consumer and geopolitical backdrop remains volatile.

Abbott Labs Bracing for Tariffs as Profits Rise

Abbott Labs boosted its earnings in the first quarter and hinted that its full-year guidance might have gotten a lift were it not for looming cost hikes from the recent tariff war.

Shipping Tycoons Discuss Separating Panama Ports From Hutchison Deal

Mediterranean Shipping Co. explores parallel tracks for contested Panama Canal ports and the rest of the deal with BlackRock.

data.title: Vin's Holdings Swings to Loss in H2 2024
data.description:
Vins's booked a loss before tax of SG$370,000 for the half ended Dec. 31, 2024, from a profit of SG$1.4 million a year earlier, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Loss per share came in at SG$0.0055 compared with earnings per share of SG$0.034 in the comparable period.

Revenue was up 11% year over year to SG$55.2 million from SG$49.6 million, mainly due to an increase in sales in the after-sales services segment.

data.title: ResMed to Maintain Dominant Sleep Apnea Device Market Share, Expected to Deliver Net Cash Balance Sheet, Jarden Research Says
data.description:
ResMed continues to dominate in obstructive sleep apnea devices globally, while delivering margin upside and a net cash balance sheet, Jarden Research said in a note on Tuesday.

The company is forecast to post fiscal third quarter non-GAAP net profit after tax of AU$362.9 million, rising past 15% from the previous corresponding period. The analysts also adjusted their diluted earnings-per-share revisions of negative 0.7%, negative 0.7%, and negative 0.8% in fiscal 2025, fiscal 2026, and fiscal 2027, respectively, to reflect a decrease in US devices revenue growth forecast to 4% from 9%.

ResMed is well-positioned amid increasing trade tensions because of its manufacturing capabilities in less sensitive geographies to the US, unlike its peers, Jarden said. Even as its US devices revenue growth is expected to slow quarter-over-quarter, it continues to dominate market share.

Masks continue to demonstrate double-digit growth for the third quarter of over 11%, with the firm continuing to maintain market share.

Jarden maintained its overweight rating on the firm and lowered the 12-month price target to AU$39.97 from AU$41.48 to reflect its earnings forecasts revisions as well as updates to its risk-free rate assumptions.

ResMed's shares were up almost 1% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Aurelia Metals Target Price Raised 3% to A$0.31/Share by Macquarie>AMI.AU
Palm snaps three-session losing run on strong rival vegetable oils
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Thursday, after hitting their lowest close since October 1 in the previous session, supported by strength in rival vegetable oils and a weaker ringgit.

The benchmark palm oil contract for July delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 25 ringgit, or 0.62%, to 4,040 ringgit ($915.89) a metric ton by 0232 GMT.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) was up 0.08%, while its palm oil contract gained 0.32%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) (BOc2) rose 0.69%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* The Malaysian ringgit, the contract currency of trade, weakened 0.05% against the U.S. dollar on Wednesday. A weaker ringgit makes the contract more attractive for foreign currency holders.

* Malaysia has maintained its May export tax for crude palm oil at 10% and lowered its reference price, a circular on the Malaysian Palm Oil Board website showed on Tuesday.

* Exports of Malaysian palm oil products for April 1-15 are estimated to have risen between 13.6% and 17% from a month ago, said cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services and independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia.

* Oil prices extended gains on the day on the prospect of tighter supply after the U.S. imposed further sanctions to curb Iranian oil trade and as some OPEC producers pledged further output cuts to compensate for pumping above agreed quotas.

* Strong crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

MARKET NEWS

* Asian stocks wavered after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned of the risk of slowing growth and rising prices due to tariffs, while the uncertainty around U.S. trade policies kept the dollar rooted near three-year lows.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

1230 US Housing Starts Number Mar

1230 US Initial Jobless Clm 12 Apr, w/e

1230 US Philly Fed Business Indx Apr

1315 EU ECB Refinancing Rate, ECB Deposit Rate Apr

($1 = 4.4110 ringgit)

data.title: EIX Investors Have the Opportunity to Lead the Edison International Securities Fraud Lawsuit with Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP
data.description:
Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses In Edison To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you purchased or acquired securities in EDISON between February 25, 2021through February 6, 2025 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

[You may also click here for additional information]

NEW YORK, April 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, a leading national securities law firm, is investigating potential claims against Edison International (“Edison” or the “Company”) and reminds investors of the April 21, 2025 deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action that has been filed against the Company.

Faruqi & Faruqi is a leading national securities law firm with offices in New York, Pennsylvania, California and Georgia. The firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors since its founding in 1995. See www.faruqilaw.com.

As detailed below, the complaint alleges that the Company and its executives violated federal securities laws by making false and/or misleading statements and/or failing to disclose that: (1) Edison’s claim that Southern California Edison Company (“SCE”) used its Public Safety Power Shutoffs ("PSPS") program to “proactively de-energize power lines to mitigate the risk of catastrophic wildfires during extreme weather events”, was false; (2) this resulted in heightened fire risk in California and heightened legal exposure to the Company; and (3) as a result, Defendants’ statements about Edison’s business, operations, and prospects, were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all times.

On January 13, 2025, a complaint was filed in the Superior Court of the State of California for the County of Los Angeles alleging that the fires originated from Edison’s power lines. The complaint included eye-witness accounts and photographs that showed the fire was started by Edison’s electrical equipment.

On this news, Edison share prices dropped by $7.73, or approximately 11.89%, on January 13, 2025

On February 6, 2025, The Wall Street Journal reported that SCE “submitted two letters to the California Public Utilities Commission with updates on its analysis of the Eaton and Hurst wildfires, saying it believes its equipment may be associated with the start of the Hurst fire.”

On this news, Edison share prices dropped by $1.28, or approximately 2.4%, on February 6, 2025.

The court-appointed lead plaintiff is the investor with the largest financial interest in the relief sought by the class who is adequate and typical of class members who directs and oversees the litigation on behalf of the putative class. Any member of the putative class may move the Court to serve as lead plaintiff through counsel of their choice, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision to serve as a lead plaintiff or not.  

Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP also encourages anyone with information regarding Edison’s conduct to contact the firm, including whistleblowers, former employees, shareholders and others.

To learn more about the Edison International class action, go to www.faruqilaw.com/EIX or call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, on X, or on Facebook.

Attorney Advertising. The law firm responsible for this advertisement is Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP (www.faruqilaw.com). Prior results do not guarantee or predict a similar outcome with respect to any future matter. We welcome the opportunity to discuss your particular case. All communications will be treated in a confidential manner.

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/f08ef15c-387e-4214-8ce6-30cbdada61ea.

James (Josh) Wilson Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP

James (Josh) Wilson Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP

data.title: US 10-Year Yield Holds Steady as Powell Signals Patience
data.description:
The yield on the US 10-year Treasury note held above 4.3% on Thursday, steadying after three consecutive days of declines, as investors weighed Federal Reserve policy signals against a backdrop of evolving trade dynamics.

In a speech on Wednesday, Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that tariffs could stoke inflation and dampen economic growth, complicating the central bank’s dual mandate of price stability and full employment.

He also indicated the Fed is in no hurry to cut interest rates, emphasizing the need for greater clarity before adjusting policy.

Meanwhile, investors awaited signs of potential trade negotiations between the US and China, with Beijing reportedly open to talks under certain conditions.

On the data front, US retail sales surged in March, posting the strongest gain in over two years, underscoring consumer strength despite mounting economic headwinds.

data.title: Indian benchmarks may open lower after Wipro's bleak outlook
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes are set to open lower on Thursday dragged by information technology stocks after Wipro forecast a decline in revenue for the April-June quarter amid tariff turmoil.

Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 23,385.5 as of 8:19 a.m. IST, indicating a 0.2% fall for the Nifty 50 from its Wednesday's close of 23,437.2.

The U.S.-listed shares of Wipro , India's fourth-largest IT company, slipped 3.2% after it missed fourth-quarter revenue estimates and warned that its revenue may drop 1.5% to 3.5% in the current quarter.

Bigger rival Infosys' U.S.-listed shares also slipped 2.6% overnight. The company will announce its earnings after Indian market hours on Thursday.

The blue-chips Nifty 50 and Sensex have risen 4.6% and 4.3%, respectively, in the last three sessions, and recovered the losses induced by April 2 tariffs, which now stand paused.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) bought Indian shares worth 39.36 billion rupees ($459.8 million) on Wednesday, as per provisional data, marking a second straight day of buying.

While U.S. President Donald Trump paused sweeping reciprocal tariffs on most countries for 90 days, the trade war with China continued to intensify.

MSCI's Asia ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was flat on the day, following a 1% drop in the previous session.

Wall Street equities fell overnight as Nvidia warned about steep charges from new U.S. curbs on its chip exports to China and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said U.S. economic growth appears to be slowing.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Angel One's fourth-quarter profit tanked 49% as tighter regulations and weak equity markets hit trading volumes

** Tata Steel's unit signs deal for liquid hydrogen import corridor between Oman, Netherlands and Germany

** Waaree Renewable Technologies posts 82.7% jump in profit for January-March quarter, but its margins drop to 26.51% from 27.55% a year ago

MSNBC INTERVIEW WITH REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX)
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 16, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX)

MSNBC INTERVIEW WITH REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX)

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

MSNBC INTERVIEW WITH REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX)

APRIL 16, 2025

SPEAKERS:

REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX)

SYMONE SANDERS TOWNSEND, MSNBC HOST

SYMONE SANDERS TOWNSEND, MSNBC HOST: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency continues its assault on the federal government, the DOGE staffers visiting the headquarters of the National Labor Relations Board today.

NBC News witnessed three young men in business attire, one of whom introduced himself as Justin, being greeted by the board's chairman and general counsel. This comes just one day after a whistle-blower alleged in a statement that DOGE caused a security breach at the NLRB and may have illegally removed sensitive data.

The whistle-blower is a cybersecurity specialist and he made the allegations — and they made the allegations in a sworn declaration submitted to members of Congress and spoke about coming forward with my colleague Rachel Maddow.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

DANIEL BERULIS, NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS BOARD WHISTLE-BLOWER: It's really to the point of where I feel like there's a culture of fear in the government right now that we're trying to combat. And by coming forward and standing up, even with this happening, I feel like fear and apathy are the death of democracy.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Joining me now is a member of the House Oversight DOGE Subcommittee, Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett of Texas.

I see you shaking your head. I mean, I thought it was very chilling. Like, he not only came forward as a whistle-blower. He's on national television, understanding that a target has likely just been put on his back. But fear and — he talked about fear and apathy.

Congresswoman, just your take on the latest DOGE assault that we are now seeing.

REP. JASMINE CROCKETT (D-TX): Yes, what I really want people to understand is that this has been painted as Democrats are for waste, fraud and abuse, and it's because they are the swamp and that kind of stuff.

And I got news for you. The only fraudsters that we see is the one that was actually convicted of fraud in a court, the one who also was found liable as it relates to his businesses for fraud. Him and his goons are the problem in this country right now.

And it is so sad when you have a government worker who literally knows that he's going to have a target on his back, but he believes in something bigger than himself. He believes in the ideals of this country. He believes in democracy. And he knows what that means. He knows that it's more than waving a flag and saying that you're a patriot, but it's coming forward and doing the right thing.

And, right now, the only thing that I'm asking of Americans, no matter what party affiliation you normally associate with, is to decide that you're going to be on the side of right. And, right now, everything about this administration is wrong.

SANDERS TOWNSEND: I would note that Elon Musk has a history with the NLRB. It's an independent federal agency. And there were questions and complaints from his employees at SpaceX in 2022.

So this — it all continues to seem to be connected.

CROCKETT: I don't know if...

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Please.

CROCKETT: Yes.

And I don't think that it was just questions or complaints. We know that he was under investigation. And when it came down to the inspector generals, the people that are supposed to make sure that they can be the watchdogs, the impartial watchdogs, this was definitely one of those watchdogs that he wanted to get rid of almost immediately.

So we don't have the inspector general that would have been overseeing that investigation nor any of the investigations around his trashy Teslas either.

SANDERS TOWNSEND: You know, this is not just — what DOGE's doing is not a Beltway-D.C. story, as I think some people on FOX News and some of our Republican friends would like to believe.

Even Marjorie Taylor Greene was asked about this in one of her town halls just recently. I want you to take a listen. She read the question, though. She didn't take a live question. We will see it.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

REP. MARJORIE TAYLOR GREENE (R-GA): "Why is MTG supporting Musk and DOGE and the slashing of Medicaid, Social Security offices, libraries and more? This is outrageous" from Sarah.

Well, Sarah, unfortunately, you're being brainwashed by the news that you're watching.

(LAUGHTER)

(END VIDEO CLIP)

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Congresswoman.

CROCKETT: This is coming from someone who clearly reads on a first-grade level, so I don't know why she's trying to clown Sarah, who obviously is a constituent of hers.

And what's really sad is, this is the woman that oversees the Subcommittee on DOGE. And when you think about the things that we should be investigating, we have had to have hearings about whether or not we should get rid of "Sesame Street," instead of her calling for, say, a hearing over something like this, where there is a concern about the fact that we may have Russian infiltration.

And I don't even know if it's infiltration at this point, because it seems like this administration wants to cozy up to Russia in all ways. And so, for all I know, they just gave over the login information, because it seems like they really haven't decided that Russia isn't our friend. Instead, they have done things like gone after Canada and Mexico.

So it is unfortunate that we are talking about issues that have to do with our safety, that have to do with our privacy. And we have an administration that I honestly don't know if they're in cahoots or not.

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Congresswoman, I have about 30 seconds left, but I don't — I'm sure you got to hear a bit of my conversation with David Hogg.

The line right now that is developing within the party are people who are willing to fight and people who don't want to take some extreme tactics. What's your take on the situation?

CROCKETT: Listen, one of the things that I was really happy about was when I looked to see how Q1 worked out for certain people.

And what we saw is that I had a historic quarter, along with AOC, along with Bernie, along with Senator Chris — I'm blanking right now.

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Chris Murphy.

CROCKETT: But I wanted — yes, Chris Murphy.

And so what we have seen is that those that were willing to be out front, be vocal and go head to head with this administration, we can see that the grassroots people, the people on the ground, we weren't getting a ton of money from big donors. I mean, on average, we have small donors that were pouring into us.

So I will say that there is agreement that, when it comes down to the base and what they're looking for is, they're looking for people that will not roll over and act like it's business as usual.

SANDERS TOWNSEND: Representative Jasmine Crockett, thank you very much.

END

data.title: South32's Alumina Production Up in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
South32's alumina production at its Worsley and Brazil operations rose in the fiscal third quarter ended March 31, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company produced 941,000 tonnes of alumina at Worsley, up 2% from 927,000 tonnes produced in the year-earlier period. Alumina sales totaled 910,000 tonnes, up 2% from 895,000 tonnes a year earlier.

Brazil alumina production reached 324,000 tonnes, up 4% from 313,000 tonnes. Sales climbed 17% to 323,000 tonnes from 277,000 tonnes.

The company maintained its alumina production guidance for the fiscal year. It lowered its Cannington output guidance by 10% due to challenging geotechnical conditions and reduced operator availability.

South32's shares were up over 1% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Transurban Group Says Group Average Daily Traffic Increased in March Quarter
data.description:
Transurban Group said its group average daily traffic (ADT) for the March quarter increased by 1.8% from a year earlier, averaging 2.5 million trips per day, helped by traffic growth in all regions, except Queensland, due to impacts from Cyclone Alfred, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

ADT increased the fastest in the company's North America business, which recorded a 3.8% growth to 152,000 trips from a year earlier, the filing said.

data.title: Mermaid Maritime Expands Fleet with Four WROVs; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Mermaid Maritime subsidiary Mermaid Subsea Services purchased four newbuild Argus electric work-class remote-operated vehicles (WROVs) from Norway-based Argus Remote Systems.

The WROVs are scheduled for delivery during the third quarter of the year, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Total consideration for the acquisition is around 11 million euros and is part of efforts to expand the group's global footprint.

Shares of the company were up nearly 4% in recent trading.

data.title: Malaysia Aviation Group reports $12 million net profit in 2024
data.description:
Malaysia Aviation Group, the parent company of Malaysia Airlines, on Thursday reported a net profit of 54 million ringgit ($12.24 million) for its 2024 financial year.

Malaysia Airlines has struggled to recover from two deadly disasters in 2014, and last year temporarily reduced its flight capacity by 18% after it was investigated by Malaysia's civil aviation authority, which found several significant safety and maintenance issues.

"Despite the capacity cuts, passenger traffic remained robust in the premium segment with stronger load factors from both passengers and cargo segment," MAG managing director Izham Ismail told a media briefing.

MAG said the capacity cuts in the fourth quarter of 2024 were driven by supply chain disruptions that extended maintenance times and delays in new aircraft delivery.

Malaysia Airlines was delisted in 2014. Sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional then took over ownership of the restructured parent company, MAG.

In 2023 MAG reported its first net profit after interest and tax since the 2015 restructuring.

MAG has been steadily growing and renewing its fleet.

Last month it said it would buy 18 737 MAX 8 and 12 737 MAX 10 aircraft from Boeing , with an option to purchase a further 30 jets.

Izham said MAG was committed to renewing its aging fleet, but that operational disruptions remained a challenge.

"Global supply chain disruptions and delivery delays have impacted the pace," he said.

The MAG group includes Malaysia Airlines, short-haul airline Firefly, and pilgrimage airline Amal.

($1 = 4.4110 ringgit)

data.title: Rupee set for modest uptick on possible inflows, wobbling dollar
data.description:
The Indian rupee is poised to inch up at open on Thursday, supported by prospects of foreign equity inflows and a dollar that remains on the backfoot on account of U.S. tariff-related uncertainty.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated that the rupee will open at 85.62 to 85.64 against the U.S. dollar compared with 85.6775 in the previous session.

The Indian currency has climbed more than 1% over the last three sessions. Foreigners have bought more than $1 billion of Indian shares over the past two days.

"The dominant theme right now is the weaker dollar, and the rupee is tagging along,” a currency trader at a bank said. He noted that sentiment on the interbank market has shifted decisively toward selling USD/INR on upticks.

The dollar index slid 0.8% on Wednesday, extending its April decline to more than 4%, and hovered near its lowest level in nearly three years.

The dollar has fallen out of favour with investors amid mounting uncertainty over U.S. trade policy. The continued back-and-forth over tariffs has weakened confidence in U.S. assets and clouded the near-term economic outlook.

Robust U.S. retail sales data released on Wednesday did little to help the dollar. U.S. March retail sales rose by the most in over two years, thanks largely to households rushing to purchase big-ticket items ahead of expected tariff-driven price increases, per ING Bank.

ING warned that with consumer confidence plummeting on price, job and wealth concerns, the consumer will be less of a growth engine later in the year.

Meanwhile, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Wednesday acknowledged the economy appeared to have slowed last quarter, noting "inflation is likely to go up" on account of tariffs.

Despite the expected rise in inflation, investors are betting on more than three Fed rate cuts this year, anticipating that growth concerns will outweigh inflationary pressures.

KEY INDICATORS:

** One-month non-deliverable rupee (INRNDFOR=) forward at 85.81; onshore one-month forward premium at 18 paise

** Dollar index at 99.44

** Brent crude futures up 0.7% at $66.3 per barrel

** Ten-year U.S. note yield at 4.31%

** As per NSDL data, foreign investors bought a net $793.4 million worth of Indian shares on April 15

** NSDL data shows foreign investors sold a net $6.9 million worth of Indian bonds on April 15
